message.content:
In /home/nick/work/libnn, run:
1. `git log --oneline -50` to get recent commits
2. `git log --oneline --all | grep -E "(opt|conv|pool|vec|rvv|vd4dots|gemm|im2col)" | head -40` to get optimization-related commits
3. `git show 0a65e6c7 --stat` for the runtime dispatch commit
4. `git show d5c5a8b7 --stat` 
5. `git log --format="%H %s" --follow Source/ConvolutionFunctions/ 2>/dev/null | head -30`

Return all output verbatim.

content.text: I'll run these git commands to show you the recent commit history and optimization-related changes.

44e47ebe [bug-fix] Fix the variable type bug in the plain C algorithm of lut_linear_s16.

commit 0a65e6c757a55e879c13fd50a40bd48f837e7b48
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 25 17:29:59 2026 +0800

    [opt/rvv] Add VLEN-portable runtime dispatch for conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any
    
    Select between VQMACC tiling (large N) and VD4DOTS direct (small N) at
    runtime based on VLMAX. Threshold = VLMAX_e8_m4 / 4, scaling automatically
    with VLEN (128 at VLEN=1024, 64 at VLEN=512, etc.).
    
    Fixes two regressions from dc9f3d62 where VD4DOTS was slower:
    - Test 9 (N=2050): now uses tiling path, matching baseline (~21M cycles)
    - Tests 1-8 (N<=32): keep VD4DOTS speedups (2.6x-26x vs baseline)
    
    QEMU verified at VLEN=128/256/512/1024, FPGA verified at VLEN=1024.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 ...v_nn_conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any.c | 231 ++++++++++++++++-----
 1 file changed, 180 insertions(+), 51 deletions(-)

input.description: Show stats for LMUL/register optimization commit

content.content:
commit d5c5a8b7965b825029635905bf32bb3980302bb1
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Mon Apr 13 11:59:00 2026 +0800

    [misc] optimize skill: move LMUL/register utilization to algorithm level
    
    LMUL selection and vector register utilization (elements per iteration,
    register blocking) are algorithmic decisions — they affect loop structure
    and throughput, not just pipeline scheduling. Micro-arch level now treats
    LMUL and algorithm as fixed, focusing only on scheduling/stalls.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 .claude/skills/optimize/SKILL.md | 11 ++++++-----
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

## 3. Runtime dispatch commit stats (`git show 0a65e6c7 --stat`)
```
commit 0a65e6c757a55e879c13fd50a40bd48f837e7b48
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 25 17:29:59 2026 +0800

    [opt/rvv] Add VLEN-portable runtime dispatch for conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any
    
    Select between VQMACC tiling (large N) and VD4DOTS direct (small N) at
    runtime based on VLMAX. Threshold = VLMAX_e8_m4 / 4, scaling automatically
    with VLEN (128 at VLEN=1024, 64 at VLEN=512, etc.).
    
    Fixes two regressions from dc9f3d62 where VD4DOTS was slower:
    - Test 9 (N=2050): now uses tiling path, matching baseline (~21M cycles)
    - Tests 1-8 (N<=32): keep VD4DOTS speedups (2.6x-26x vs baseline)
    
    QEMU verified at VLEN=128/256/512/1024, FPGA verified at VLEN=1024.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 ...v_nn_conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any.c | 231 ++++++++++++++++-----
 1 file changed, 180 insertions(+), 51 deletions(-)
```

## 4. LMUL/register optimization commit stats (`git show d5c5a8b7 --stat`)
```
commit d5c5a8b7965b825029635905bf32bb3980302bb1
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Mon Apr 13 11:59:00 2026 +0800

    [misc] optimize skill: move LMUL/register utilization to algorithm level
    
    LMUL selection and vector register utilization (elements per iteration,
    register blocking) are algorithmic decisions — they affect loop structure
    and throughput, not just pipeline scheduling. Micro-arch level now treats
    LMUL and algorithm as fixed, focusing only on scheduling/stalls.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 .claude/skills/optimize/SKILL.md | 11 ++++++-----
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 5 -)
```
